summary: The query asks whether T-cell deficiency affects spatial learning after toluene exposure. While toluene impairs spatial learning through neurotransmitter disruptions and white matter injury, no mechanism connects T-cell deficiency—primarily an immune defect—to these specific neurological processes. Therefore, based on current evidence, this interaction does not occur.
